title: bot/scripting: deadlock-safe pipe drain before wait() is correct
description: bot/src/scripting.rs:55-80: spawning stdout and stderr drain tasks before calling child.wait() is the correct pattern to prevent OS pipe buffer deadlock (> ~64 KiB output blocks the child on write, wait() blocks on child exit — neither can proceed). The comment explains exactly why. A future contributor might 'simplify' by moving the reads after wait() — this creates a latent deadlock for hook scripts with large output. The pattern must stay.
